slides.title: Getting Started with Kissflow
slides.subtitle: Account setup, navigation, and your first steps on the platform

slides.title: Signing In and Account Access
bullets: Access Kissflow via your organization's unique account URL
Sign in with corporate SSO, Google, or email credentials
Account admins manage users, groups, and roles from Admin panel
Multi-factor authentication supported for enterprise security
Mobile apps available for iOS and Android
PWA browser access for mobile without app installation
slides.notes: Demo the login flow and account URL structure.

slides.title: Home Page and Navigation
bullets: Home page shows apps, processes, and boards you have access to
Left navigation: Home, My Tasks, Admin, App Store, and more
My Tasks — centralized inbox for all pending approvals and actions
Global search finds processes, apps, items, and documentation
Quick create button (+) to start new process items or access builders
Notification bell for real-time updates on assigned items
slides.notes: Walk through the UI live — this is critical for new users.

slides.title: My Tasks — Your Work Inbox
bullets: All pending approvals, reviews, and inputs in one unified inbox
Filter by process, app, priority, due date, and status
Bulk approve or take action on multiple items where permitted
Delegate tasks to colleagues when you are unavailable
Mobile notifications ensure you never miss a critical approval
Completed tasks move to history with full audit trail
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > User guide > Global My Tasks

slides.title: User Roles and Permissions
bullets: Super Admin — full account control and governance settings
Process Admin — manage specific processes and their settings
App Admin — build and publish apps within assigned scope
User — initiate items, complete assigned tasks, view permitted data
Custom roles defined per process, app, board, or dataform
Group-based permissions simplify large organization management
slides.notes: Explain role hierarchy — who can build vs who can use.

slides.title: Community Documentation Map
bullets: 🎬 Getting started — Onboarding, Developer guide, Mobile guide
📒 User guide — Apps, Processes, Boards, Forms, Integrations, Analytics
🔎 Quick references — FAQs, Glossary, Discontinued services
💼 Troubleshooting — Tips, tricks, and common issue resolution
Product Updates — Announcements, roadmap, pre-release notes
565+ documentation posts — searchable and categorized

slides.title: Developer Guide Essentials
bullets: Developer guide under Getting started in Community
Covers JavaScript SDK, custom scripting, and API patterns
Service accounts for secure API authentication
Authentication keys management in Admin settings
Links to developers.kissflow.com and api.kissflow.com
GitHub resources for SDK examples and samples
slides.notes: For technical trainees — point to Developer Hub.

slides.title: Mobile Guide Highlights
bullets: Native iOS and Android apps for task management on the go
Submit forms, approve items, and track progress from mobile
Push notifications for new assignments and SLA breaches
Offline form draft support (where configured)
PWA option for browser-based mobile experience
Mobile guide in Community covers setup and troubleshooting

slides.title: First Week Action Plan
bullets: Day 1: Explore home page, complete profile, review My Tasks
Day 2: Submit a test item in an existing process
Day 3: Browse App Store templates relevant to your department
Day 4: Enroll in Kissflow Academy — Workflow Automation course
Day 5: Join Community, follow categories, introduce yourself
Week 2: Shadow a process builder or attend advanced training
slides.notes: Give trainees a concrete plan to follow after this session.

slides.title: Support and Help Resources
bullets: In-app support chat for technical and billing questions
Community forums for peer-to-peer help and best practices
Kissflow Academy for structured self-paced learning
Kissflow Pulse Podcast for industry trends and tips
Professional services for complex implementation projects
Status page for platform uptime and incident updates
slides.notes: Wrap up Getting Started — open floor for questions.
